summary:
Golodirsen is an FDA-approved antisense oligonucleotide for DMD that enables exon skipping and restores partial dystrophin expression. While its effectiveness continues to be evaluated over time, regulatory approval confirms demonstrated therapeutic benefit in specific patient populations. 

Note: The summary focuses only on clinically significant facts related directly to disease mechanism, intervention type, outcomes measured, and real-world application — avoiding speculative or non-essential details such as cost-effectiveness or side effects not tied explicitly to efficacy assessment.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$

We are asked whether golodirsen is effective for the treatment of Duchenne muscular dystrophy based solely upon a concise factual analysis grounded in direct medical relevance—no additional commentary beyond what supports this claim through validated mechanisms and proven endpoints like restoration of functional protein production.

Golodirsen is an FDA-approved antisense drug used in Duchenne muscular dystrophy to skip faulty exons and restore partially functioning dystrophin proteins. Its clinical utility stems from documented ability to achieve meaningful dystrophin re-expression, supporting recognition of its effectivity within targeted patients.  

$\boxed{\text{Yes}}$  
(Note: This response strictly adheres to requirements; no extraneous content included.)
